post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 294: Genshin Mafia
Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not refer to private conversations with the moderator in any way unless given permission.
    • 1.5: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.6: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.7: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.8: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.9: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.10: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.11: Confirm by listening to a song from Genshin Impact and posting in your role pm your analysis of the musical quality of the song and your feelings when listening to it.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/code]
      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I fdas am the moderator of Mafia Game 294
    • 5.2: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.3: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.4: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.5: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Events
Day 1: mathjay hanged
Night 1: ad333 died
Day 2: falliinqsnow- hanged
Night 2: scannose died
Day 3: ostriches88 hanged
Night 3: shari11, Socoobo, tigeryong died
Day 4: WisteriaV hanged


PM Prods
 


Warnings
 


Story
Atop the snowy peaks of Dragonspine, a mysterious being escaped from where they had been trapped for so long. He observed the humans within in the city of Mondstadt, how they lived their carefree lives each day, smiling, laughing, and having fun. A life that he himself could not have. Yet his counterpart lived among the people as one of them, living an undeserved life which was stolen from him.

Now, a group of adventurers were climbing the mountain, seeking treasure or whatever it was that humans sought. Soon, he would take his place among them, and they would be none the wiser. A gust of wind filled the air with a flurry of snow, filling the area with white. When the snow dissipated, the unknown figure was gone.


Do not post until this line is crossed out.

Day 1 begins at 01:00:00 EDT 12 August 2024.

OK SOOOOOOOOOO
If we're goign by the story of the modnstat arc in game, then yes this is somewhat accurate. The main thing for me is taht the Fatui don't play all that big a role, there's only one important one La Signora and she only comes in at the end, and is more important in a later section. They do have a hotel there i guess? In the past Il Dottore did some evil experiments on Collei but both of those are more important later on & this is only revealed in the manga.

The Abyss order however... The main plot is that this dragon Dvalin is evil, but Venti is secretly the god of the area and knows that dvalin is nice, and as i turns out he is corrupted by Some abyss mage i forgot idk they're not that important. Later on, Dainsleif comes back aand reveals the eyee of the first field tiller of smth to be here which also has connections to the abyss order, but quite frankly they're boring.

The third main "faction" is the treasure hoarders, which has more hinting at them in flavor, given the people who are guessed to be hunting for treasure, however they are even blander than the first two factions besides when there's individual members.

Now for the Weeeeeeeird lore. So Dragonspine (which was explicitly mentioned), not only was the home of an ancient civilization (probably irrelevant) but it's also where a different dragon Durin is dead, however he was recently hinted to be coming back to life. However he too thinks himself to be good so where does that leave us??

Well Albedo lives in dragonspine too, and not only that his evil clone was there somehow (idk this part i wiki'd). So that could be an option. However, Albedo's creator (he's not human), Rhinedottr is part of the morally gray group the Hexenzirkle including Alice & Barbeloth

It's also worth noting that a while in the past the city of Mondstadt was ruled by some evil people but idt that's what's going on.


tl;dr maybe but they're also like literal no-names most of the time unless i'm forgetting something

post_rendered:
I mean it's not about posting more it's about "helping the town out" as a result of the playerlist.

Option 1) This is a standard tigeryong believes should apply to everyone but doesn't want to force

This is fine, a bit weirdly worded.

Option 2) Specifically referring to town's ability to read him/his ability to read specific players

I mean specify but sure fine

Option 3) tiger specifically thinks that his play will affect whether or not town reads this game.

First off, his would confirm some other things about tiger's playstyle. Second, I'm going to try my best not to attack tiger as a player, but let's just keep in mind that he self-rated experience near the very bottom.

Town play is at its core not based around one individual. Sure there are players who lead the town, particularly in certain playerlists, but they can die during the night, and what are you left with then? Furthermore no player is perfect, and the best solves only come from listening to everybody. And conversely, your average town shouldn't be trying to control the thread unless it's remarkably stagnant (which this playerlist is ~unlikely to do) and even then the goal is to revitalize the thread and throw it back in the hands of the people. Mafia is a game about not being able to trust anybody but it is just as much a game about needing to trust everybody.

Option 4) There was no real version of this.

post_rendered:
imagine if i just look back at this in 8 hours and don't understand my logic anymore
basically i think one trait that is noticeable among the three most lhf slots of this game (shari, k12, tiger) is that all of them are playing to some extent within their worlds (and there's nothing wrong with it, it just shows that they haven't fully incorporated themselves into the thread yet) - for instance, k12's early game solving was mainly in the form of isos, and shari decided to do a "read of the day" thing.

there's nothing implausible about these mindsets coming from town, and being the most comfortable way they'd want to play the game in - though faking them also isn't difficult (shari's case is debatable), and in some circumstances they may choose to do so as wolf.
specifically, i think k12's mindset is the least alignment indicative; on the other hand, shari and tiger seem frozen at times, which isn't something i think they would do as wolf
(and also imo k12 is slowly getting out of this state by the latter half of this dayphase, which is why im being less lenient towards him being underwhelming.

... i still don't know why snow would do what she is doing as scum, but maybe i should start raising my expectations towards townies.
which im inclined to attribute it to some of the prior games i've had; especially 284, which i pushed like everyone for being underwhelming once and finally settled on the powerwolf (which turned out to be correct)
and recently i have been very frequently wrong for just calling people twtbaw
and it has been tilting me very much
maybe i should just be asking the question "why would they do this as town?" question more. and this probably wouldn't solve everything - it's part of the reason i was tunneling k12 - but i hope i'd figure out how to balance things eventually

i could probably connect some of these topics to more general themes irl, but im not feeling as emotional as i was now so i don't feel like doing it anymore.


now that i've written it out, i realized it'd be less useful than it was in my mind, but again i'll try to figure things out in the couple few days - and maybe also learn not to be tilted so much that it affects doing homework.

@k12 - the next time you get online, i'd like to say something to you. and it wouldn't be shading you or interrogating you, i can promise that.

post_rendered:
more than half a month ago, i opened my role pm, seeing first the sweet, town green; then "Kaeya" and recognizing the ability to rb people
a thousand futures flashed in front of my eyes and i entered thread, feeling relatively stoked

but of course, i couldn't shake away that pr mindset
it lurked in every corner, and when i sent that game breaking misvote in 18/6
the thread seemed to crumble down
posts blurred and my energy completed sapped away
it was so simple, yet came so fast

facing wolfreads from every corner i decided
maybe it would feel better to adjust myself
i posted through the dayphase, trying to hide from confronting the reality: my reads? nonexistent
my motivation? gone

and it seemed to work! mathjay died and without a blink the 3 days during the night were the most eventful. just time away from accusing glares helped me relapse back into towny mindset
without thinking too much about my night actions, i set it on nd and went into a period of meditation

by chance, i opened thread the moment sod started and realized... maybe all that towny energy finally decided to come back
i shot out posts after posts, giving evaluations despite still having not read the majority of the thread
but thats okay! i didnt need to read the entire thread, i realized
that change in mindset was groundbreaking

until fate decided the redcheck would've been a suitable obstacle to set me back
and i tried to face it head on. probability aint that simple yall.
but no. townies and wolfies alike decided to turn away, i suppose

it was happening again. i could recognize it in any thread, and there wasn't anything i could do.

just stuck there
in that dark hole
being buried
i tried to speak to the outside world

...silence.

post_rendered:
When the sun arose, the adventuring party roused themselves from their sleep and began checking if anything had happened during then night. That is when they discovered that scannose had gone missing. A frantic search began as they looked for the missing adventurer. Finally, someone discovered him in a snowbank. He lay at the bottom of a steep cliff nearby the camp, having fallen to his death during the night. If they had been less well informed, they may have thought it was an accident, but they knew better now. Upon investigation of the body, they found that scannose was an ordinary Citizen

Day 3 begins. Current time: 21:00:00 EDT 11 September 2024.

post_rendered:
k12 being a wolf makes sense if you think about it from the perspective of a busser
you need to get the most towncred out of the push while also making it so that living doesn’t make you suspicious
and a pj with a redcheck optimizes both of those

it’s universally agreed upon that someone getting a redcheck and their target flipping wolf makes them town
and the 2sbp of the pj claim makes it so that scum would naturally hesitate to use their nk on them so he could live and us not bat an eye
and the rng aspect means that he doesn’t have to keep giving info

it’s literally the perfect claim + bus if you know that one of your teammates is going to stay in the poe no matter what and quite possibly die that day regardless of whether you fake the redcheck

it’s brilliant
and i’m lowkey okay with losing to it if that’s how it goes

A bizarre mimetic plant monster that harnesses the elements in combat.
These plants lurk under the surface and ambush unsuspecting prey. With the passage of years, sufficient nutrition, and the right environment, it could even take root somewhere and grow into a giant tree that sways and spews elemental power...

Whopperflowers are dangerous plants that can be found all over Teyvat. Whopperflowers often disguise themselves as Sweet Flowers, Mints or Small Lamp Grasses in the wild, ambushing the player if they try to pick up the fake plant.

Whopperflowers possess higher than average RES to all damage types. In combat, they use aggressive elemental attacks and can burrow underground to quickly reposition. They will also periodically generate an elemental shield while charging up and unleash a special attack if the shield is not destroyed in time. If the shield is destroyed, they will instead become paralyzed with reduced RES.

post_rendered:
primordial albedo page wrote:
Primordial Albedo was the first version of Albedo made by Rhinedottir as a product of the Primordial Human Project, but was deemed a failed experiment and was eventually swallowed by Durin. Many years after Durin's death, Primordial Albedo escaped the dragon's body, taking Dragonspine as his haunt. Seeing Albedo live among humans, Primordial Albedo grew envious that a synthetic being was able to fully enjoy the human experience and began shadowing Albedo, concocting a plan to replace him.
flavor wrote:
Atop the snowy peaks of Dragonspine, a mysterious being escaped from where they had been trapped for so long. He observed the humans within in the city of Mondstadt, how they lived their carefree lives each day, smiling, laughing, and having fun. A life that he himself could not have. Yet his counterpart lived among the people as one of them, living an undeserved life which was stolen from him.

Now, a group of adventurers were climbing the mountain, seeking treasure or whatever it was that humans sought. Soon, he would take his place among them, and they would be none the wiser. A gust of wind filled the air with a flurry of snow, filling the area with white. When the snow dissipated, the unknown figure was gone.

Atop the snowy peaks of Dragonspine, a mysterious being escaped from where they had been trapped for so long. He observed the humans within in the city of Mondstadt, how they lived their carefree lives each day, smiling, laughing, and having fun. A life that he himself could not have. Yet his counterpart lived among the people as one of them, living an undeserved life which was stolen from him.

Now, a group of adventurers were climbing the mountain, seeking treasure or whatever it was that humans sought. Soon, he would take his place among them, and they would be none the wiser. A gust of wind filled the air with a flurry of snow, filling the area with white. When the snow dissipated, the unknown figure was gone.

Atop the snowy peaks of Dragonspine, a mysterious being escaped from where they had been trapped for so long. He observed the humans within in the city of Mondstadt, how they lived their carefree lives each day, smiling, laughing, and having fun. A life that he himself could not have. Yet his counterpart lived among the people as one of them, living an undeserved life which was stolen from him.

Now, a group of adventurers were climbing the mountain, seeking treasure or whatever it was that humans sought. Soon, he would take his place among them, and they would be none the wiser. A gust of wind filled the air with a flurry of snow, filling the area with white. When the snow dissipated, the unknown figure was gone.

Primordial Albedo was the first version of Albedo made by Rhinedottir as a product of the Primordial Human Project, but was deemed a failed experiment and was eventually swallowed by Durin. flavor wrote:
Atop the snowy peaks of Dragonspine, a mysterious being escaped from where they had been trapped for so long. Yet his counterpart lived among the people as one of them, living an undeserved life which was stolen from him.

Suddenly, the Traveler heard a shout that caught their attention. They turned and saw Albedo running toward them. Their eyes widened with surprise. As they turned back to the one they were talking to, a potion splashed against them, freezing them in place. Then, a sword stabbed through their chest and out their back. They looked at the face of the one who attacked them, the face of Albedo, though they now noticed was slightly off.

Behind them, the true Albedo was caught in a block of ice which trapped them to the ground while a monster towered over him. The Traveler had fallen, and now they were next.

"Goodbye," the false Albedo said. "Now, all that you have shall belong to me."

Congratulations to NDMath (Susbedo), falliinqsnow- (Cryo Whooperflower), and K124659 (Cryo Whooperflower) for winning the game.

post_rendered:
The mafia team made some very bold gambles that paid off well, which put them in control of the game from start to finish. But the only reason they were able to pull this off was because of town's misplays. Too many town PRs claimed or heavily softed, which allowed the mafia team to neutralize them with roleblocks and kills. I personally think that PRs should not soft in this way. If other town can pick up on the soft then so can mafia. They are just handing info to the mafia and giving them an advantage. And while ostriches and Socoobo didn't soft, they relied on their PR status as a crutch and decided to play poorly, leading to them being put on the chopping block and being forced to claim. And on day 2, Scipio's nudge nudge wink wink succeeded only in telling mafia to roleblock Wisteria.

Having all of this information let mafia know how to use their night actions to maximum effect. ostriches didn't need to be roleblocked because they looked at his reads and determined that he was unlikely to investigate snow or k12 and would probably investigate ND. ad was outing himself as a mason partner so he gets killed. Then night 2 they knew exactly who to roleblock to stop town from getting more info. All of that because town was handing out information for free. Mafia's biggest blunder came on night 3 though where they almost threw the game by killing off the easy mislims and narrowing down the POE too much. They dodged a bullet though when shari didn't use their ability. If shari did use their ability then mafia would probably have lost because 2 kills + all town being roleblocked implies that ND is mafia. But at the same time town dodged a bullet when mafia roleblocked Msv's kill of Wisteria, which would have ended the game outright.

So overall, credit to the mafia team for pulling off the plays they did, though they were heavily enabled by town's mistakes.

fdas wrote:
The mafia team made some very bold gambles that paid off well, which put them in control of the game from start to finish. But the only reason they were able to pull this off was because of town's misplays. Too many town PRs claimed or heavily softed, which allowed the mafia team to neutralize them with roleblocks and kills. I personally think that PRs should not soft in this way. If other town can pick up on the soft then so can mafia. They are just handing info to the mafia and giving them an advantage. And while ostriches and Socoobo didn't soft, they relied on their PR status as a crutch and decided to play poorly, leading to them being put on the chopping block and being forced to claim. And on day 2, Scipio's nudge nudge wink wink succeeded only in telling mafia to roleblock Wisteria.

Having all of this information let mafia know how to use their night actions to maximum effect. ostriches didn't need to be roleblocked because they looked at his reads and determined that he was unlikely to investigate snow or k12 and would probably investigate ND. ad was outing himself as a mason partner so he gets killed. Then night 2 they knew exactly who to roleblock to stop town from getting more info. All of that because town was handing out information for free. Mafia's biggest blunder came on night 3 though where they almost threw the game by killing off the easy mislims and narrowing down the POE too much. They dodged a bullet though when shari didn't use their ability. If shari did use their ability then mafia would probably have lost because 2 kills + all town being roleblocked implies that ND is mafia. But at the same time town dodged a bullet when mafia roleblocked Msv's kill of Wisteria, which would have ended the game outright.

So overall, credit to the mafia team for pulling off the plays they did, though they were heavily enabled by town's mistakes.

I think one of the problems here was lack of consideration of "Why let ostriches88 get the check" unless if you are sure there are better PRs and you know who they are
and as for our n3 plan, we were debating on keeping in scb and tiger however it agreed upon for a doc dodge and extra towncred
a bad LYLO could have also won the game, and it was a gamble
